title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
brute_force_explanation: One intuitive approach is to merge the two arrays, sort the merged array, and then find the median. However, this would result in a time complexity of O((m+n)log(m+n)) which is not efficient.
brute_force_steps: [{"description":"Create a new array to hold the merged elements","line":1,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":0},"values":{"sum":0,"target":0},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[]}},{"description":"Merge the two arrays","line":2,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":0},"values":{"sum":1,"target":1.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1]}},{"description":"Merge the remaining elements","line":3,"state":{"pointers":{"left":1,"right":0},"values":{"sum":3,"target":2.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1,2]}},{"description":"Find the median","line":4,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":0},"values":{"sum":3,"target":2.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1,2]}}]
edgeCases: ["m == n and both arrays are sorted in ascending order","One array is empty or contains only one element"]
examples: [{"input":"[1,3],[2]","output":2,"explanation":"Merged array is [1,2,3], and the median is 2."},{"input":"[1,2],[3,4]","output":2.5,"explanation":"Merged array is [1,2,3,4], and the median is (2 + 3) / 2 = 2.5."}]
optimal_explanation: We can use binary search to find the median by dividing the merged array into two halves and comparing their medians. If the median of the left half is less than or equal to the target, we move the right pointer; otherwise, we move the left pointer. This approach reduces the time complexity from O((m+n)log(m+n)) to O(log(min(m,n))).
optimal_steps: [{"description":"Divide the merged array into two halves","line":1,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":6},"values":{"sum":7,"target":3.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1,2,2]}},{"description":"Find the median of the left half","line":2,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":3},"values":{"sum":6,"target":3.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1,2,2]}},{"description":"Compare the median of the left half with the target","line":3,"state":{"pointers":{"left":0,"right":3},"values":{"sum":6,"target":3.5},"array1":[1,3],"array2":[2],"array":[1,2,2]}}]
problem_statement:
Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays.

The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).

 

Example 1:

Input: nums1 = [1,3], nums2 = [2]
Output: 2.00000
Explanation: merged array = [1,2,3] and median is 2.

Example 2:

Input: nums1 = [1,2], nums2 = [3,4]
Output: 2.50000
Explanation: merged array = [1,2,3,4] and median is (2 + 3) / 2 = 2.5.

 

Constraints:

  • nums1.length == m
  • nums2.length == n
  • 0 <= m <= 1000
  • 0 <= n <= 1000
  • 1 <= m + n <= 2000
  • -106 <= nums1[i], nums2[i] <= 106
secondaryPatterns: ["Optimizing database merges","High-frequency trade matching","Real-time sensor data fusion"]
shortPatternReason: Partitioning search space to find the middle element in logarithmic time.
